videos.title: The $6,000,000 Wholesaling Business Powered by AI | Winc. Podcast
videos.description:
Nick Perry breaks down how he scales a nationwide wholesaling operation using market data, pending percentages, and AI-powered lead management. He explains how automation helps his team generate more offers, qualify leads faster, and handle massive deal flow.

Nick also covers novations, compliance, renegotiations, and the systems needed to manage high-volume transactions. The episode highlights how AI, strong KPIs, and clear team roles are reshaping modern real estate investing.

Nicks Mentorship: https://www.7figurecartel.com/
Nicks Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/nickperryrei/reels/
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
When only the best will do, Brent recommends👇

💸 BEST INBOUND LEADS: https://www.talktopeople.com/lp/yt/

🛠️ BEST ALL-IN-ONE SOFTWARE (includes unlimited skip tracing!!): https://dealmachine.com/ttp

*Motivated seller lists, cash buyer lists, skip tracing, direct mail, CRM, property data and COMPs, repair/rehab and offer calculators, driving for dollars app, and more!

🚀 GET A CUSTOMIZED BLUEPRINT for getting started in wholesaling: https://go.wholesalinginc.com/fp/custom-blueprint/

🦏 BEST TRAINING, COACHING and COMMUNITY for real estate investors: https://www.wholesalinginc.com/mentorship/ (the ‘Rhino Tribe’)

Brent Daniels went from broke at 30, to multi-million dollar real estate investor in less than 5 years! Today he’s the nation’s leading expert on wholesaling, and has taught thousands of investors how to get started and build the business and lifestyle of their dreams! He lives with his family in Phoenix, Arizona, and still owns and operates a 7-figure per year wholesaling business. 

Since 2013, Wholesaling Inc. has been the leader in real estate investing education. It’s podcast, the “Wholesaling Inc. Podcast,” is the most listened to podcast in the wholesaling space.

insights.summary: Nick Perry details how he built a $6M nationwide wholesaling operation using AI-powered lead management, market data analysis (pending percentages), and structured team roles. The episode covers deal structures including novations, renegotiations, and compliance considerations. Key emphasis is on KPI-driven performance, AI automation for lead qualification, and high-volume transaction management.
key_lessons: Use pending percentage as a primary market selection metric — higher pending rates signal stronger buyer demand and faster deal flow, guiding where to focus acquisition resources
AI lead management can handle initial call handling and lead qualification at scale, allowing human closers to focus only on motivated, pre-qualified sellers

Novations are a core deal structure for situations where a traditional wholesale assignment isn't optimal — understanding when to pivot between wholesale, novation, or flat-fee listing is a competitive edge
KPIs and offer generation volume are the north star metrics — tracking offers made per rep per day drives predictable deal flow and holds the team accountable
Clear organizational roles (lead manager, acquisitions/closer, disposition) with defined targets per position prevents bottlenecks and scales deal flow without chaos
trends: AI automation for inbound/outbound lead handling and qualification is becoming a standard infrastructure layer for high-volume wholesaling operations
Novations are gaining traction as a compliance-friendly alternative to traditional assignments, especially as regulations around wholesaling tighten nationwide
Nationwide market expansion driven by data (pending percentages, market conditions) rather than geography-first thinking is reshaping how wholesaling businesses scale
acquisition_one_liners: I'm not an agent — I'm a direct buyer. I can close fast, as-is, no repairs, no showings. Does that sound like something that would help your situation?
We work with sellers who need flexibility — whether that's a quick close, cash offer, or even listing on the market if that gets you more money. What matters most to you right now?
I just want to understand your situation better — is the priority getting the highest price possible, or is it more about speed and certainty of closing?
A lot of sellers we work with are in a position where the traditional route just takes too long or costs too much. Is that something you're running into?
We can structure this a few different ways depending on what works best for you — can I ask a few quick questions to see which option fits your situation?
disposition_one_liners: We have a property under contract in [area] — as-is, clear title, seller motivated. I can send you the details now. Are you actively buying in that market?
This one is priced to move fast — we're looking for a cash buyer who can close in 14 days or less. Do you have the capacity to close quickly if the numbers work?
We JV on deals regularly if you have buyers in markets we're working — we split the fee and you handle the buyer side. Want to talk about how we can structure that?
tips_and_tricks: Track pending percentage per market weekly — a rising pending rate signals increasing buyer demand and is a leading indicator for where to push more acquisition activity
Use AI call handling for initial lead response and intake so no lead goes cold — human closers should only engage after AI pre-qualifies motivation level and timeline
Separate the closer and disposition roles clearly — closers should never be distracted by buyer-side negotiations; that kills offer output and KPI performance
Build renegotiation into your standard process, not as an exception — inspect, find issues, and train your team to renegotiate before closing as a margin protection tactic
When facing compliance risk on assignments, pivot to a novation structure — seller lists with you as agent/facilitator, you find the buyer, and you earn the spread legally
insights.south_florida_relevance: No specific mention of Miami-Dade, Broward, Palm Beach, or Florida markets in the description or chapter titles. However, the novation compliance discussion is directly relevant to South Florida — Florida has increasingly scrutinized wholesale assignment practices, making novations a tactically important structure for Buying Hero to understand and potentially adopt. The pending percentage market selection framework is also immediately applicable to analyzing Miami-Dade sub-markets.

videos.title: Seller got a cash offer for $120k but signed with me for $88k!
videos.description:
Got a seller lead.
.
Went on appointment.
.
Seller had already received multiple offers (highest was $120k).
.
Signed with us for $88k.
,
Why?
.
He didn’t trust the other buyers.
.
Never forget - In distressed real estate. Certainty is more important than price!
,
BTW - this was a $30 ppl lead provided by @ispeedtolead .
.
Ispeedtolead Special Offer:
Pay $500, get $500 free credits 
https://jerry.ispeedtolead.com

insights.summary: A seller with a competing cash offer of $120K signed a contract for $88K — $32K less — because they trusted the wholesaler more than the other buyers. This case study reinforces that distressed sellers prioritize certainty and trust over maximum price. The lead was sourced via iSpeedToLead at $30 per lead, highlighting cost-effective motivated seller marketing.
key_lessons: Certainty beats price with distressed sellers — a seller accepted $32K less because they trusted the buyer to close
Your credibility and rapport on the appointment can outweigh competing higher offers — focus on building trust, not just price
Distressed sellers fear being ghosted or deals falling through — position yourself as the certain, reliable option explicitly on every call and appointment
iSpeedToLead is generating motivated seller leads at ~$30/lead — a trackable, scalable cold lead source worth testing against current marketing
Going on appointments (in-person or high-touch) creates a trust advantage over buyers submitting offers remotely or via text
trends: Sellers receiving multiple cash offers is increasingly common — trust and certainty positioning is a key differentiator in a competitive offer environment
Pay-per-lead platforms (like iSpeedToLead) are being used by top wholesalers as a complement to cold calling and direct mail
Seller psychology in distressed real estate continues to favor reliability over top dollar — a durable trend worth building scripts around
acquisition_one_liners: I want to be upfront with you — I'm not here to give you the highest number and then back out. I'd rather give you a number I can actually close on and show up when I say I will.
A lot of sellers we talk to have already gotten offers that never turned into anything. We close on the date we agree on, period.
The highest offer doesn't always mean the best offer. What matters is who actually shows up to the closing table.
I know you may have other offers — that's fine. What I can promise you is certainty. No surprises, no renegotiating at the last minute.
We're not a big hedge fund or a random investor — we're local, we close fast, and you'll have my number the whole time. That's what makes us different.
disposition_one_liners: This one's already under contract — seller was motivated, took below market, and we've got a clean title situation. Closing in [X] days, assignment fee is [X].
I've got a deal I'm moving fast on — seller needed certainty more than price, so we locked it up well below comps. Let me know if you want the numbers.
This is a trust-based deal — seller passed on higher offers to work with us. That tells you the contract is solid and the seller won't be difficult at closing.
tips_and_tricks: Train your acquisitions reps to explicitly verbalize certainty on every seller call — say 'we close on the date we set, no renegotiating' as a standard close
When a seller mentions they have other offers, don't panic on price — pivot to trust and certainty language immediately rather than going higher
Track cost-per-lead by source — this deal came from a $30 iSpeedToLead lead, which is a useful benchmark to compare against your current cold call and direct mail CPL
Use case studies like this (seller took $32K less for certainty) as internal training stories for your lead manager and acquisitions manager to internalize and retell on calls
On appointments where competing offers exist, bring proof of recent closings, testimonials, or a track record — physical credibility markers reinforce the certainty narrative
insights.south_florida_relevance: No specific mention of South Florida, Miami-Dade, Broward, or Palm Beach markets. However, the trust-over-price dynamic is highly applicable to Miami-Dade where sellers frequently receive multiple unsolicited offers from iBuyers, hedge funds, and other wholesalers — making certainty positioning a critical differentiator for Buying Hero's acquisitions team.

Here's EXACTLY How to Find Houses to Flip (FAST & FREE)!

If you're trying to figure out how to find houses to flip (fast and without spending money on tools or marketing), this video shows you the exact steps to do it. 

These strategies work in any city, with no special access, software, or cold calling.

🏠 Want To Become An Ultimate Investor & Close 3-10+ Real Estate Deals a Month? Book a FREE Strategy Session with our team here to see if you're the right fit for our Ultimate Investor Program:

In this step-by-step video, Ryan Zomorodi, Co-Founder of RealEstateSkills.com, shows you our go-to strategies for finding fix and flip deals using a completely FREE tool, including how to use platforms like Redfin, Zillow, and Realtor.com to locate fixer-uppers and investment properties anywhere in the U.S!

You'll see how to use free tools like Redfin to locate discounted homes, fixer-uppers, and undervalued deals that most new house flippers miss.

These tools will help you make smarter offers, avoid bad deals, and become a more confident investor.

insights.summary: This video covers free MLS-based deal-finding strategies using Redfin, Zillow, and Realtor.com, focusing on keyword searches, price reductions, and days-on-market filters to identify distressed and motivated seller listings. While framed around fix-and-flip, the lead generation and property identification tactics are directly transferable to wholesaling acquisition pipelines. No transcript was available, so analysis is based on the video description and timestamps only.
key_lessons: Search MLS platforms using keywords like 'cash,' 'investment,' 'TLC,' 'as-is,' and 'fixer-upper' to surface motivated seller listings without paid tools
Filter by longest days on market (DOM) to find sellers who are likely fatigued and more open to discounted offers — high-value target for cold follow-up
Sort listings by lowest price per square foot to quickly identify undervalued properties relative to the surrounding market
Use Redfin's built-in fixer-upper filter as a free, fast screener for distressed inventory that other buyers overlook
Track price reduction history on listings — repeated reductions signal a motivated seller who may be open to a wholesale-price offer
trends: Free MLS-based sourcing is gaining traction as an alternative to expensive list-pulling tools like PropStream or BatchLeads
On-market distressed deals are becoming more competitive as more investors discover MLS keyword filtering tactics
Longer days on market is emerging as a primary motivated seller signal in a cooling or normalized market environment
acquisition_one_liners: I noticed your property has been on the market for a while — has anything changed with your situation or timeline?
I saw the price has come down a few times. I work with cash buyers and can close fast — would a quick, as-is offer be something you'd consider?
We specialize in properties that need work, so condition is never an issue for us. Can you tell me more about what's going on with the house?
I'm not an agent, I'm an investor — so there are no commissions, no repairs, no showings. We just close and you move on.
A lot of sellers I talk to just want certainty and speed over top dollar. Is that something that matters to you right now?
disposition_one_liners: I have a fixer-upper under contract in [area] — priced below market with strong ARV upside. Sending details now, let me know if you want to take a look before I move to the next buyer.
This one checks all the boxes: distressed property, motivated seller, and room for your rehab margin. Can you move quickly if the numbers work?
I pulled comps and the spread is there — I'll send you the deal sheet and the ARV breakdown so you can verify it yourself.
tips_and_tricks: Use Redfin's 'fixer-upper' filter combined with a DOM sort (highest first) as a daily 10-minute prospecting routine to build a free motivated seller list
Screenshot or save listings with multiple price reductions — these are warm leads you can approach directly if FSBO, or track for expired listing follow-up
Cross-reference MLS keyword hits ('cash only,' 'sold as-is,' 'estate sale') with county probate or tax delinquency records to stack motivation signals
Run a price-per-sqft comparison against 3-5 nearby recently sold properties to quickly gut-check whether a listing has wholesale spread before spending time on it
Build a simple tracker of long-DOM listings and follow up every 2 weeks — seller motivation increases with time and many wholesalers abandon follow-up too early
insights.south_florida_relevance: No specific mention of South Florida, Miami-Dade, Broward, or Palm Beach markets. The strategies are presented as market-agnostic and applicable nationwide. However, MLS keyword filtering and DOM targeting are highly applicable to the Miami-Dade market where distressed listings often carry 'cash only,' 'as-is,' or estate-related language due to probate activity and older housing stock. Team should apply these filters on Redfin/Zillow specifically within Miami-Dade zip codes as a supplemental free lead source.

videos.title: The MASSIVE Real Estate Shift Happening Right Now
videos.description:
A major shift is happening in real estate right now, and most investors still haven’t caught on. In this episode, Thach Nguyen explains how accessory dwelling units (ADUs) are changing the way investors create value and generate cash flow from a single property. By adding additional units to existing homes, investors can turn one deal into multiple rental properties while forcing appreciation and building long-term wealth. Thach breaks down how this strategy works, where the opportunity is emerging across the country, and how investors can find properties with the lot size and layout needed to make it work. He also shares how market cycles, zoning changes, and housing shortages are creating the conditions for this strategy to take off.
 
Thach's ADU Training: https://learn.springboardtowealth.com/adu

insights.summary: This video covers the ADU (Accessory Dwelling Unit) strategy where investors add units to existing properties to force appreciation and generate rental cash flow. The guest Thach Nguyen discusses finding properties with adequate lot sizes, navigating zoning changes, and using market cycles to identify opportunity. While not a wholesaling-specific video, the ADU angle is relevant for identifying a motivated seller niche and understanding what end buyers are hunting for.
key_lessons: ADU-eligible properties (large lots, zoning-friendly areas) represent a hidden value layer that most sellers don't know they're sitting on — creating a motivated seller opportunity
Focus on B and C neighborhoods where lot sizes are larger and zoning is more flexible — these overlap heavily with distressed seller demographics
Lot size and layout are the primary filters for ADU deal viability — train your team to screen for this on inbound leads
Zoning changes and housing shortages are accelerating ADU legalization across markets — properties that couldn't be split before may now qualify
Understanding what end buyers (buy-and-hold investors adding ADUs) want allows wholesalers to position deals more effectively and justify higher assignment fees
trends: ADU legalization is expanding nationwide as cities address housing shortages, opening new value-add angles for investors
B and C neighborhood properties with large lots are being re-evaluated for their development potential beyond just ARV
Market clock positioning — investors are being advised to buy now during uncertainty to capture the next cycle upswing
acquisition_one_liners: I know the market feels slow right now, but that's actually why I'm able to offer you a fair cash price quickly — most buyers are sitting on the sidelines.
A lot of sellers in your area don't realize the lot size alone adds value — I can make you an offer based on what the property is actually worth to an investor.
We buy properties as-is, so you don't need to worry about repairs or updates — we handle all of that after closing.
I'm not a realtor, so there are no commissions or fees — what we offer is what you walk away with.
Even if the house needs work, the land and location still have value to us — can you tell me a little more about the property?
disposition_one_liners: This lot is large enough to potentially add an ADU — for a buy-and-hold investor, that's a second income stream baked into the deal.
We're seeing strong demand from landlords looking for value-add plays in B and C neighborhoods — this one checks both boxes on lot size and location.
The ARV is solid, but the real upside here is the lot — a buyer who knows the ADU angle could be looking at significantly more cash flow than a standard rental.
tips_and_tricks: When screening inbound seller leads, add a quick lot size check — anything over 6,000-7,000 sqft in a zoning-flexible area could have ADU upside to pitch to your buyers list
Build a buyer persona for ADU investors specifically — they underwrite deals differently (income-based, not just ARV) and will pay more for the right lot
Use zoning map tools or county GIS systems to pre-screen your driving-for-dollars or direct mail lists for ADU-eligible parcels before spending marketing dollars
When presenting deals to cash buyers, include lot dimensions and current zoning in your deal summary — ADU-aware buyers will self-select and move faster
Focus cold call and direct mail campaigns on older single-family homes in B/C neighborhoods with large lots — high overlap with distressed/motivated seller profile
insights.south_florida_relevance: No direct mention of Miami-Dade, Broward, or Palm Beach. However, South Florida is highly relevant to this strategy: Miami-Dade and Broward have been expanding ADU ordinances, housing shortage is acute, and B/C neighborhoods with large lots (Liberty City, Opa-locka, parts of Hialeah, West Palm) align perfectly with Buying Hero's existing acquisition targets. The team should cross-reference their current lead list against county zoning updates for ADU eligibility as an added disposition angle.

daily_themes: AI automation for inbound/outbound lead handling and qualification is becoming a standard infrastructure layer for high-volume wholesaling operations
Novations are gaining traction as a compliance-friendly alternative to traditional assignments, especially as regulations around wholesaling tighten nationwide
Nationwide market expansion driven by data (pending percentages, market conditions) rather than geography-first thinking is reshaping how wholesaling businesses scale
Sellers receiving multiple cash offers is increasingly common — trust and certainty positioning is a key differentiator in a competitive offer environment
Pay-per-lead platforms (like iSpeedToLead) are being used by top wholesalers as a complement to cold calling and direct mail
Seller psychology in distressed real estate continues to favor reliability over top dollar — a durable trend worth building scripts around
top_lessons: Use pending percentage as a primary market selection metric — higher pending rates signal stronger buyer demand and faster deal flow, guiding where to focus acquisition resources
AI lead management can handle initial call handling and lead qualification at scale, allowing human closers to focus only on motivated, pre-qualified sellers

Novations are a core deal structure for situations where a traditional wholesale assignment isn't optimal — understanding when to pivot between wholesale, novation, or flat-fee listing is a competitive edge
KPIs and offer generation volume are the north star metrics — tracking offers made per rep per day drives predictable deal flow and holds the team accountable
Clear organizational roles (lead manager, acquisitions/closer, disposition) with defined targets per position prevents bottlenecks and scales deal flow without chaos
Certainty beats price with distressed sellers — a seller accepted $32K less because they trusted the buyer to close
Your credibility and rapport on the appointment can outweigh competing higher offers — focus on building trust, not just price
Distressed sellers fear being ghosted or deals falling through — position yourself as the certain, reliable option explicitly on every call and appointment
